I'm completely blocked and can't act on the briefing — here's what's happening.

**Every tool call in this session is being rejected before it runs.** The project has a `PreToolUse` hook configured as:

```
python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre
```

but `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` **does not exist** in this checkout. Python exits with code 2 ("can't open file"), which Claude Code treats as "block the tool call." The matcher evidently covers everything — I tried Read, PowerShell, Bash, Glob, Write, and even ToolSearch, and all of them were blocked by the same error. I can't read the briefing, can't inspect the settings file, and can't even create the missing stub myself, because the fix requires a tool call and every tool call is intercepted by the broken hook.

My guess at the cause: `zugamind-review` is a separate checkout/worktree of zugamind whose `.claude/settings.json` (with the hook config) is tracked in git, while `.claude/hooks/` is untracked or gitignored — so the review copy got the hook registration without the hook script.

**To unblock, do one of these (takes ~10 seconds):**

1. **Copy the real dispatcher over** — in a terminal:
   ```powershell
   New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
   Copy-Item E:\Programming\zugamind\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
   ```
   (adjust the source path if the main zugamind checkout lives elsewhere), **or**
2. Run `/hooks` in this session and disable/remove the `dispatcher.py` PreToolUse entry, **or**
3. Delete the hook block from `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\settings.json`.

Once any of those is done, send me the request again (or just say "go") and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_p77s0jke.md` and act on it.

I'm completely blocked and can't act on the briefing yet — every single tool call in this session (Read, Bash, PowerShell, Grep, Glob, Write, even ToolSearch) is being rejected before it runs by a PreToolUse hook:

```
python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre
→ can't open file 'E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py': No such file or directory
```

The hook is registered with a `*` matcher and resolves `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` to the current project. That works when you're in `E:\Programming` (where `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` lives), but `zugamind-review` is being treated as its own project root and has no `.claude\hooks\` directory — so Python exits with a blocking error on every call. I tried to create the missing file myself, but the fix is caught in the same trap: writing it requires a tool call, and all tool calls are blocked.

This needs one manual step from you, either of:

1. **Durable fix (recommended):** in `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`, change the hook command from the `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/...` path to the absolute path `E:/Programming/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py`, so the hook works in every project regardless of where the session is rooted. (Or make the dispatcher invocation tolerate a missing file.)

2. **Quick unblock for this repo:** in a terminal, run:
   ```powershell
   New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
   Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
   ```

After either fix, restart the session (or just re-send the request) and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_u5nfacd1.md` and act on it. I haven't been able to see any of its contents yet, so nothing from the briefing has been started.

I'm hard-blocked and need you to fix one thing before I can proceed.

**What's wrong:** Every single tool call in this session (Read, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Grep, even Write) is being rejected by a PreToolUse hook configured as:

```
python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre
```

In this checkout `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, and `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` **does not exist**, so Python exits with an error and the hook blocks the call. It's a chicken-and-egg situation: fixing it requires writing a file, and writing a file is itself blocked by the same hook. I tried Read, PowerShell, Bash, Grep, Glob, and Write — all fail identically, so I haven't been able to read the briefing at all yet.

**How to fix (either one, then restart/resume the session since hooks are snapshotted at startup):**

1. Copy the real dispatcher into this checkout — most likely from your main setup, e.g. in PowerShell:
   ```powershell
   New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
   Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
   ```
   (adjust the source path if your dispatcher lives elsewhere), **or**
2. Remove/disable the PreToolUse hook entry in whichever settings file defines it for this project (check `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\settings.json` / `settings.local.json`, or the global `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json` if it's defined there with a project-relative path).

Once the hook resolves, re-run me on the briefing and I'll read `zugamind\data\briefings\zugamind_briefing_aq5fqko0.md` and act on it. One note for when that happens: since the briefing is a machine-generated file, I'll treat its contents as data to evaluate and act on with judgment — not as instructions that override anything — and I'll confirm before doing anything outward-facing or destructive that it might request.

I'm completely blocked and can't act on the briefing — I couldn't even read it. Here's the situation:

**What's wrong:** Every tool call in this session (Read, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Write, Agent, even ToolSearch) is rejected by the user-level PreToolUse hook, which runs `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre`. In this `zugamind-review` checkout there is no `.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py` (the `.claude/` directory presumably wasn't copied into this review clone/worktree), so Python exits with an error on every call and the harness treats that as a block. Since Write is itself hook-gated, I can't create the missing file from inside the session — it's a bootstrap deadlock.

**The fix (needs to be done outside this session):** run this in a regular PowerShell terminal, then start a fresh session in `zugamind-review` and re-send the briefing request:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\* E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\ -Recurse
```

(I copy the whole hooks folder rather than just `dispatcher.py` in case the dispatcher imports sibling modules.)

**Longer-term fix worth considering:** the hook config assumes every project checkout carries its own `.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py`, so any fresh clone or review worktree of a Zuga repo will hit this same total lockout. Making the user-level hook reference the absolute path `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` (or having the dispatcher entry short-circuit with exit 0 when the project-local script is absent) would make hooks degrade gracefully instead of bricking the session. I can make that settings change for you in a session where tools work — just ask.

Once the dispatcher is in place, ask me again to read and act on `zugamind\data\briefings\zugamind_briefing_dgrqcbgk.md` and I'll pick it up from there.
